summary: Matched a block of Bowser/Koopa fighter code by moving many routines from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftkoopa.s into src/melee/ft/chara/ftkoopa.c, adding needed Fighter struct fields and callback prototypes, and updating assembly action-table/function-pointer references from lbl_* labels to func_* symbols. The PR had no body or review comments, so intent beyond matching more ftKoopa functions is inferred from the diff.
what_changed: Removed roughly 525 lines of ftKoopa assembly and replaced them with about 210 lines of C implementations in src/melee/ft/chara/ftkoopa.c.

Added C implementations for func_801330E4, func_8013319C, func_8013322C, func_801332C4, func_80133324, func_80133398, func_801333F8, func_80133484, func_801334E4, func_8013359C, func_80133654, func_80133690, and func_801336CC.
func_801330E4 was promoted from an extern in ftkoopa.c to a real C function and declared in src/melee/ft/chara/ftkoopa.h.
Updated ftKoopa action/callback pointer references in asm/melee/ft/code_8008521C.s from lbl_80133654/lbl_80133690/lbl_801336CC to func_80133654/func_80133690/func_801336CC.
Updated remaining ftkoopa.s references from lbl_* targets to newly decompiled func_* symbols such as func_80133484, func_801334E4, and func_8013359C.
Extended Fighter layout in src/melee/ft/fighter.h by exposing x894 as f32, x914 as f32, and typing x1A58 as HSD_GObj* instead of u32; surrounding filler ranges were adjusted to preserve offsets.

The decompiled ftKoopa code uses Fighter state variables including x2200_ftcmd_var0, x2340_stateVar1, x2344_stateVar2, x2348_stateVar3, and x234C_stateVar4 to select animation/action IDs and reset command/state flags.

The new C preserves behavior around action changes through func_800693AC, callback setup through func_8007E2D0, animation/callback helpers such as func_8007E2F4/func_8007E2FC, and linked object handling through ft->x1A58.
smart_moves: The PR did not only add C bodies; it also fixed the data model needed for matching by exposing exact Fighter offsets used by the assembly, notably x894, x914, and x1A58.

Typing x1A58 as HSD_GObj* made the new C calls to func_800DC920, func_800CC730, func_800BCE64, and func_800BC9C8 line up naturally with object-pointer usage instead of carrying an integer placeholder.
Renaming action-table pointers from lbl_* to func_* kept assembly tables linked to the newly emitted C symbols and avoided leaving stale label references after moving functions out of asm.
The code kept address-based func_* names rather than inventing semantic names, which is appropriate when the diff evidence only proves matching and not final behavior names.
State reset patterns were kept explicit after action changes, for example clearing x2340_stateVar1, x2348_stateVar3, and x2200_ftcmd_var0 in the same places as the assembly.
The implementation preserved small control-flow quirks such as temporarily negating x2C_facing_direction around action changes when x234C_stateVar4 is set.
decomp_lessons: When decompiling fighter character code, matching often depends on adding precise struct fields instead of continuing to use filler arithmetic; here x894 and x914 were introduced because the ftKoopa routines read them directly.
If an asm label is used as an action-state callback, converting the function to C requires updating every table entry or wrapper reference from lbl_* to func_* so relocation targets remain correct.
Address-named functions can still meaningfully improve the codebase by shrinking asm and exposing control flow, even before semantic names are known.

C implementations for fighter action transitions should mirror the original ordering: fetch user_data, call animation/action helpers, then reset state variables/callback flags exactly where the assembly did.
For linked/attached objects stored in Fighter fields, first establish the correct pointer type in fighter.h; that can reduce casts and make calls involving child/held objects match more directly.

Unused-looking temporaries such as the added double unk/unk1 variables appear in several routines; the slice gives no comment explaining them, but they are likely matching artifacts and should not be removed casually without checking codegen.
naming_conventions: Newly decompiled functions kept address-derived names in func_80133xxx form.
Raw labels used as callable routines were upgraded from lbl_80133xxx to func_80133xxx in function-pointer contexts.
Fighter state fields followed existing placeholder-offset naming, e.g. x2340_stateVar1, x2344_stateVar2, x2348_stateVar3, x234C_stateVar4, and x2200_ftcmd_var0.
Fighter struct additions followed offset-based names: x894, x914, and x1A58.
Pointer fields and local variables used HSD_GObj* where the diff showed object-pointer behavior, notably x1A58.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Large contiguous deletion from ftkoopa.s indicates a classic asm-to-C match block rather than isolated cleanup.
The C preserves magic action IDs and flags seen in assembly, including 0x15B, 0x15C, 0x15D, 0x15E, 0x15F, 0x160, 0x161, 0x162, 0x163, 0x1FF, 0x0C4C5088, 0x044C1080, 0x80080, and 0x80.
The implementation uses direct global float labels lbl_804D9AD8 and lbl_804D9ADC in func_800693AC/func_8006F0FC calls to preserve float constant references.
The new C sometimes reloads ft from gobj->user_data after calls, matching assembly patterns where user_data was reloaded after action changes or callback setup.
Callback setup through func_8007E2D0 preserves function-pointer operands such as func_801330E4 with func_800BC8D4, and func_8013302C with func_800BC7E0.
Action-state tables in code_8008521C.s were updated to point at C symbols func_80133654, func_80133690, and func_801336CC, preventing symbol mismatch after decompilation.
The added Fighter fields narrowed filler arrays around exact offsets, which is a common matching tactic when loads/stores otherwise appear as raw offset accesses.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename ftKoopa func_801330E4 or the surrounding func_8013319C-func_801336CC block to semantic Bowser move names.
Search for later changes to Fighter.x894, Fighter.x914, and Fighter.x1A58 to see whether their semantics were identified.
Check later reviews or commits involving ftKoopa action IDs 0x15B through 0x163 for naming or state-machine cleanup.
Search for other PRs updating code_8008521C.s action tables from lbl_* to func_* after character-function matches.
Investigate whether the unused double unk/unk1 locals in ftKoopa.c remained necessary for matching or were later removed after compiler/matching improvements.
